findings.description: Missing api_usage_logger instrumentation on extract_menu_data, assign_tarot, classify_photo, and summarize_reviews. Only enrich_shop logs usage. The plan acceptance criteria requires per-task cost breakdown (enrich_shop, classify_photo, embed), but 4 of 5 Anthropic LLM call sites are uninstrumented — their costs will be invisible in the spend dashboard.

Missing api_usage_logger instrumentation on extract_menu_data, classify_photo, and summarize_reviews. Only enrich_shop logs usage. Same gap as the Anthropic adapter — 3 of 4 OpenAI LLM call sites are uninstrumented.

Missing api_usage_logger instrumentation on embed() and embed_batch(). These are OpenAI API calls that incur cost (text-embedding-3-small pricing is in cost.py). The plan acceptance criteria lists 'embed' as an expected task in the spend breakdown, but embedding calls are never logged.

OpenAI usage fields prompt_tokens and completion_tokens can individually be None per the SDK types (Optional[int]). compute_llm_cost expects int arguments. If either is None, the multiplication in compute_llm_cost will TypeError. Should default to 0: tokens_input=_usage.prompt_tokens or 0.

Mock rows in test_spend_returns_aggregated_totals omit tokens_input and tokens_output fields. The endpoint aggregates these into today_tokens_in/mtd_tokens_in per task, but this path is completely untested. Adding token fields to mock data and asserting on the task-level token aggregation would improve coverage of the aggregation logic.

Apify log_api_usage passes cost_usd=None, which is correct per the design (Apify cost computed at query time from CU). However, the task is hardcoded as 'scrape_batch' even though _run_actor is a general-purpose method. If _run_actor is called from other tasks in the future, all usage will be attributed to scrape_batch. Minor because currently all Apify calls are scrape_batch.

skipped.reason: datetime.fromisoformat in loop: each row has a unique timestamp — parsing must be per-row, not hoistable. CLAUDE.md performance rule does not apply here.

Design quality review: SpendTab is an admin-only internal tool component using standard shadcn/ui Table components. No consumer-facing design violations. Spacing uses Tailwind utility classes mapped to design tokens.
